title: Compaction Strategies
learningObjectives: Derive the rate–distortion Lagrangian for joint compaction-compression, and analyze why sequential (compact-then-compress) pipelines leave bits on the table.
Quantify the per-attribute parameter budget of a full 3DGS representation and calculate the theoretical size at each SH degree, identifying where the budget is concentrated.
Evaluate SH degree reduction, rotation reparameterization, and isotropic Gaussians as attribute-reduction strategies, justifying the priority ordering given a target bit-rate and scene type.
Analyze the failure modes of standard adaptive density control and evaluate PixelGS, MCMC, and SUNDAE against the criterion of producing a representation that is both accurate and coding-friendly.
Assess the ScaffoldGS anchor architecture and HAC's hash-grid entropy model, and justify the conditions under which the anchor+MLP approach reduces total coded bits relative to flat 3DGS.

Design a compaction strategy for a specified scene type and bit-rate budget, selecting and sequencing techniques from the module, predicting compression ratios, and interpreting deviations from predicted outcomes.

readings.title: Compaction vs. Compression and Attribute Reduction
Structural Modifications: Anchors, LOD, and Improved Rendering

lab.title: Compaction Pipeline: SH Reduction, Pruning, and Size Analysis
